🌍 Earth Pulse
The other side of the mirror
Earth Pulse Index
—
—
—
Computing…

Aggregate of 34 independent signals across 9 domains — from space to oceans, internet traffic to migration flows. Score 100 = calm world, 0 = extreme stress.

⚠️ Signals under stress
Normalisation methodology
Historical percentile
Each series compared against its own 5-year history. BDI at 1400 and Kp at 7 become comparable via percentile ranking.
p = rank(v) / N × 100
Inverted polarity
Signals "bad when rising" (conflicts, pollution, food prices) are inverted so 100 = always calm.
score = 100 − percentile(v)
Weighted aggregation
Category score = weighted average of signals. EPI = average of 9 categories, conflict & disaster overweighted.
EPI = Σ(wᵢ × cᵢ) / Σwᵢ
🌍 Earthquakes — USGS live i
USGS live earthquakes
All earthquakes above the selected magnitude detected globally in the last 7 days. USGS processes seismic data from 2,000+ stations worldwide in real-time. The 20-year average shows how current activity compares historically.
Source: USGS · earthquake.usgs.gov · Real-time

🔥 Active fires — NASA FIRMS (VIIRS) i
NASA FIRMS — Fire Information
Satellite-detected active fire spots globally, updated every 12 hours via the VIIRS instrument on Suomi NPP. Covers wildfires, agricultural burns, and industrial fires. 1-year comparison shows seasonal vs. anomalous activity.
Source: NASA FIRMS · firms.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov · 12h refresh
By region
—
active fire spots
🌾 FAO Food Price Index i
FAO Food Price Index
Composite of 5 commodity groups: cereals, vegetable oils, dairy, meat, sugar. Base 100 = 2014–2016 average. The 2008 spike to 213 triggered riots in 30+ countries. The 2022 spike to 160 followed the Russia-Ukraine war. Directly correlated with political instability.
Source: FAO · fao.org · Monthly (1st Friday)
⚠️ 2008: 213 pts (food riots) · 2011: 237 pts (Arab Spring) · 2022: 160 pts (Russia-Ukraine)
🦠 Flu — WHO FluNet i
WHO FluNet
Weekly influenza surveillance from 130 countries. Source: WHO FluNet API · who.int/tools/flunet · Weekly updated. Data loaded from data/flu.json via GitHub Actions, or WHO REST API directly.
Source: WHO · services.who.int/flumart · Weekly
🌡 Global SST anomaly i
Sea Surface Temperature anomaly
How much warmer/cooler ocean surfaces are vs. the 1991–2020 average. 2023-2024 hit unprecedented +0.6°C. Directly drives hurricane intensity, coral bleaching, and global weather extremes.
Source: NOAA OISST · Daily · 0.25° resolution
+0.64
°C vs. 1991–2020
↑ +0.12 vs. last year
El Niño active
2024 record
🌊 Sea level rise i
Global mean sea level
Rise in mm vs. 1993 satellite baseline. Rate accelerated from 3.3 mm/yr (2000–2015) to 4.8 mm/yr (2020–2024). At current pace: +30–50cm by 2100, threatening 800M+ coastal residents.
Source: NASA PODAAC / Copernicus · 10-day cycle
+103
mm vs. 1993
↑ +4.1 mm/yr (accelerating)
+3.3 mm/yr 2000–2015
+4.8 mm/yr 2020–2024
💨 CO₂ Mauna Loa i
Keeling Curve — Atmospheric CO₂
Weekly CO₂ readings from Mauna Loa volcano (Hawaii) since 1958. The sawtooth pattern = forests breathing seasonally. The long-term rise = fossil fuels. 422 ppm today vs. 316 ppm in 1960 — +33% in 65 years.
Source: NOAA GML · gml.noaa.gov/ccgg · Weekly
—
ppm atmospheric CO₂
Loading…
🌀 El Niño / ONI i
Oceanic Niño Index
Temperature anomaly in the Niño 3.4 Pacific region. Above +0.5°C for 5+ months = El Niño (droughts in Australia, floods in Americas). Below -0.5°C = La Niña. Affects global agriculture, insurance losses, and tropical storms.
Source: NOAA CPC · Seasonal update
+0.8
°C Niño 3.4 anomaly
Moderate El Niño
2023–2024 episode
Forecast: neutral 2025
🌿 Keeling Curve — Atmospheric CO₂ since Mauna Loa measurements began i
Keeling Curve
Charles Keeling started measuring CO₂ in 1958. At 316 ppm then, now at 422+ ppm. The annual sawtooth = northern hemisphere forests absorbing CO₂ in summer, releasing in winter. The upward trend = human emissions. One of the most important charts in history.
Source: NOAA GML · Mauna Loa Observatory · Weekly since 1958
💨 Air quality — OpenAQ live (PM2.5 µg/m³) i
PM2.5 Air Quality Index
Particulate matter <2.5µm — the most dangerous air pollutant, penetrating deep into lungs. WHO guideline: 5 µg/m³ annual mean. Values shown are live readings from nearest monitoring station. Delta shows deviation from city's annual average.
Source: OpenAQ · api.openaq.org · Real-time · CORS enabled
🏜 Drought Monitor i
Drought Monitor
% of territory under drought conditions (D0–D4 scale). D0 = abnormally dry · D4 = exceptional drought. Directly tied to crop yields, wildfires, and water restrictions. The US Drought Monitor publishes weekly maps covering the continental US.
Source: US Drought Monitor · NOAA/USDA · Weekly
🏞 River flow — USGS (Mississippi) i
Mississippi River flow
Real-time discharge at Baton Rouge gauge (#07374000), converted to m³/s from cubic feet per second. The Mississippi drains 40% of the continental US. Low flow = drought/supply chain risk. Historical comparison shows anomalous conditions.
Source: USGS WaterWatch · waterservices.usgs.gov · 15-min intervals
—
m³/s discharge

🌊 Sea surface temperature anomaly i
Global SST anomaly
Monthly departure from 1991-2020 baseline. Positive = warmer than average. 2023-2024 broke all records at +0.64 C. Warm ocean = more hurricane energy + coral bleaching.
Source: NOAA OISST · Daily global coverage · 0.25 degree resolution

🛸 ISS — live i
International Space Station
Orbits Earth every 92 minutes at ~28,000 km/h, 408 km altitude. The ISS is the only ongoing project where Russia, USA, and Europe cooperate without interruption since 1998.
Source: wheretheiss.at · Every 8 seconds

🌌 Kp Index — geomagnetic activity i
Planetary Kp Index
Measures disturbance of Earth's magnetic field caused by solar wind. Scale 0–9. Kp ≥ 5 = minor geomagnetic storm. Kp ≥ 7 = risk to power grids in Canada/Scandinavia. Kp = 9 → auroras visible from France.
Source: NOAA SWPC · services.swpc.noaa.gov · 1-min
—
/ 9 max
Loading…
−72hNow
Kp 0–3: calm · 4–5: minor storm · 6–7: moderate · 8–9: severe → satellite/grid risk
🌠 Aurora visibility forecast
🛰 Active satellites in orbit i
Satellites in orbit
There are currently ~8,800 active satellites in orbit, of which ~6,000 are Starlink. LEO (Low Earth Orbit, <2000km) hosts the majority. The rapid growth creates collision risk — Kessler syndrome could make certain orbits unusable within decades.
Source: UCS Satellite Database · ucsusa.org · Quarterly updated
Total active
8,800+
Starlink
~6,000
SpaceX LEO
+2,877% growth since 2019 · Kessler syndrome risk in LEO
🚨 Space weather alerts — NOAA i
NOAA Space Weather Alerts
Real-time alerts from NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center. Covers geomagnetic storms, solar radiation storms, and radio blackouts. Scale: G1–G5 (geomagnetic), S1–S5 (radiation), R1–R5 (radio). G5 events can damage power grids globally.
Source: NOAA SWPC · services.swpc.noaa.gov/products/alerts.json · Real-time
Loading NOAA alerts…
🌞 Solar cycle 25 — sunspot activity i
Solar cycle 25
The Sun follows an ~11-year activity cycle. We are currently in Solar Cycle 25, which began in December 2019 and is tracking above the official NOAA prediction — a more active cycle than expected. Solar maximum predicted 2025. High activity = more flares, CMEs, and aurora events.
Source: NOAA SWPC · Solar Cycle Progression · Monthly
Sunspot number
—
vs. prediction
+32
above forecast
Cycle 25 peak predicted ~2025 · tracking well above NASA/NOAA forecast
☄️ Near-Earth objects — NASA CNEOS i
Near-Earth Objects (NEO)
Asteroids and comets passing within 1.3 AU of Earth. NASA tracks 35,000+ NEOs. Objects are classified as "potentially hazardous" if they are >140m wide AND pass within 0.05 AU. No known impact threat exists for the next 100 years.
Source: NASA CNEOS · ssd-api.jpl.nasa.gov/cad.api · Daily
Close approaches (7d)

☀️ Solar wind — NOAA DSCOVR i
Solar wind (DSCOVR satellite)
Speed, density and Bz component measured at the L1 Lagrange point, 1.5M km from Earth. Bz < −5 nT = geomagnetic storm risk. Speed > 700 km/s = elevated aurora probability. 45-minute warning before Earth impact.
Source: NOAA SWPC · DSCOVR · plasma-7-day.json · 1-min
Speed
—
km/s
Density
—
p/cm³
Bz (IMF)

nT
🔭 X-ray Solar Flux (GOES) i
X-ray Solar Flux
Solar flare classification: A (quiet) · B · C (common) · M (moderate — radio blackouts) · X (severe — GPS/grid risk). X10+ flares can disable satellites. Measured by GOES-16/18.
Source: NOAA SWPC · GOES primary · xrays-6-hour.json · 1-min
Class
—
Flux

✈️ Live air traffic — OpenSky Network i
OpenSky Network
Real-time aircraft positions from 5,000+ ADS-B receivers worldwide. Each dot = one aircraft with heading. Auto-refreshes every 30 seconds. Pre-COVID baseline: ~14,000 flights globally.
Source: OpenSky · opensky-network.org · Real-time ADS-B

🚢 Live maritime traffic — AISStream i
AISStream WebSocket
Real-time AIS (Automatic Identification System) positions streamed via WebSocket. AIS is mandatory for vessels >300 GT. Data covers cargo ships, tankers, container ships, and passenger vessels worldwide.
Source: AISStream.io · WebSocket · aisstream.io

⚓ Baltic Dry Index i
Baltic Dry Index (BDI)
Cost of shipping bulk dry cargo (coal, iron ore, grain) across 26 major routes. A leading indicator of global industrial demand — moves 4–8 weeks before official economic statistics.
Source: Stooq.com / Yahoo Finance · data/bdi.json · Weekly
⚓
Requires data/bdi.json
→ Run GitHub Actions
📦 Container freight — Freightos FBX i
Freightos Baltic Exchange (FBX)
Cost to ship a 40ft container on major routes (Asia–Europe, Asia–US). Spiked 10× in 2021 supply chain crisis. Current normalization near pre-COVID levels signals stabilization.
Source: Freightos FBX · data/fbx.json · Weekly
Current rate (est.)
~$1,940
per 40ft container

🚛 ATA Truck Tonnage — USA (FRED) i
ATA Truck Tonnage Index
Volume of goods transported by US trucks (index 2015=100). What trucks carry today appears in stores in 3 weeks. A leading indicator for US retail sales and consumer spending. The "vs. year-ago" line shows whether freight activity is accelerating or contracting.
Source: FRED · TRUCKD11 · St. Louis Fed · Monthly (6-week delay)

🌐 Global BGP routing table i
Global BGP routing table size
Number of IPv4 prefixes in the global BGP routing table. Growth indicates internet expansion. Sudden large changes indicate route leaks or hijacks. The table has grown from ~100K routes in 2000 to 950K+ today.
Source: RIPE NCC RIS · stat.ripe.net · Public API
—
IPv4 prefixes in global BGP table
Loading RIPE stat…
🔀 Active BGP incidents i
BGP routing anomalies
Border Gateway Protocol is the postal system of the internet — BGP incidents mean routes are being rerouted or withdrawn. Can signal network attacks, accidental misconfigurations, or government-ordered shutdowns. Each incident can affect millions of users instantly.
Source: IODA (Georgia Tech) · ioda.inetintel.cc.gatech.edu · Real-time
—
Routing anomalies detected
🚫 Countries with disruption i
Internet shutdowns
Countries where internet traffic dropped >30% vs. baseline. Detected by IODA (CAIDA research). Myanmar (2021 coup), Ethiopia, Iran, and Sudan have appeared here before major news coverage. Each shutdown affects millions of people.
Source: IODA (Georgia Tech) · ioda.inetintel.cc.gatech.edu · Live
—
Traffic reduction >30%
📡 Internet outage alerts — IODA / Cloudflare Radar i
Internet outage detection
IODA (Internet Outage Detection and Analysis) by CAIDA uses BGP routing data and active probing to detect connectivity disruptions. This is the same methodology used by digital rights organizations to document censorship events.
Source: IODA · ioda.caida.org · Real-time · No API key required
Loading IODA data…
🖥 Global infrastructure health i
Infrastructure health monitor
Status of major internet infrastructure providers — CDNs, hyperscalers, and critical platforms. These providers collectively serve >90% of internet traffic. A disruption at any one can cascade across millions of services.
Source: status.io · uptime monitoring · Live
⛽ EU Natural Gas Storage — AGSI+ (GIE) i
EU Gas Storage levels
% fill level of underground gas storage across EU countries. Directly correlated with winter energy prices and inflation. EU alert threshold: 20%. Winter target (set by EU regulation): 90% by November 1. The 2022 energy crisis saw Germany at 18% fill in August.
Source: AGSI+ / GIE · agsi.gie.eu · Daily · AGSI_KEY required
—
⛽
Loading AGSI+ data…
🛢 US Oil Stocks — EIA (weekly) i
EIA Weekly Petroleum Stocks
Crude oil inventory at Cushing, Oklahoma — the WTI oil price delivery point. Published every Wednesday at 10:30 AM ET. The deviation from the 5-year average is the key figure traders watch: surplus = bearish for oil price, deficit = bullish. Each million barrel = ~$0.50 price impact on WTI.
Source: EIA · api.eia.gov · Weekly Wednesdays · EIA_API_KEY required
🛢
Requires data/oil_stocks.json
⚡ EU Electricity consumption — ENTSO-E / RTE France i
EU Electricity demand
Real-time electricity consumption is the most honest GDP proxy — it shows exactly when industries stop and start, without statistical delay. ENTSO-E aggregates 35 European countries hourly. RTE France (open API, no key) is used as a proxy when ENTSO-E token not configured.
Source: RTE France open API / ENTSO-E · Near real-time
France (RTE)

ACLED events i
ACLED conflict events
Total events of political violence and protest documented globally in the last 30 days. Includes battles, violence against civilians, explosions, riots, and protests. ACLED codes events weekly from 3,000+ local, national, and international sources in 75+ languages.
Source: ACLED · acleddata.com · Daily · ACLED_EMAIL + ACLED_PASSWORD required
Last 30 days
—
Fatalities documented i
ACLED fatalities
Confirmed conflict-related deaths in the last 30 days. Includes combatants and civilians. ACLED only counts fatalities directly reported by primary sources — actual toll is likely higher. Civilian fatalities tracked separately.
Source: ACLED · fatalities field · 30 days
incl. civilians: —
—
Countries affected i
Countries with active conflicts
Number of countries with at least one documented violence event in the last 30 days. Presence here does not necessarily mean war — even a single protest counted as violence qualifies. Context from the disorder type breakdown below is essential.
Source: ACLED · 30-day global coverage
Last 30 days
—
Civilian targeting i
Civilian targeting events
Events where civilians were explicitly targeted according to ACLED classification (civilian_targeting field). A qualitative indicator of conflict severity beyond simple event counts. High civilian targeting = war crime risk.
Source: ACLED · civilian_targeting field · 30 days
Events targeting civilians
🗺 Conflict map — ACLED geolocated events (30 days) i
ACLED conflict map
Each dot = one geolocated conflict event in the last 30 days. Size proportional to fatalities. 🔴 Battles/Armed violence · 🟠 Explosions/Remote violence · 🟡 Protests/Riots. When ACLED credentials are configured, dots represent real geolocated events.
Source: ACLED · latitude/longitude fields · data/conflicts.json
🔴 Battles/Violence🟠 Explosions🟡 Protests
📊 Conflict trends i
Conflict trend analysis
Weekly: 90-day event count by week · Monthly: 12-month trend · Types: breakdown by event type · Regions: by ACLED geographic region. All views show both event count and fatalities where available.
Source: ACLED · data/conflicts.json · weekly_trend_90d · monthly_trend_12m
🌍 Top 10 countries — events (30 days) i
Country ranking by events
Top 10 countries by event volume. A country appearing here does not necessarily mean it is the most dangerous — some regions have better journalistic coverage. The fatalities column (☠) is more indicative of conflict severity than event count alone.
Source: ACLED · 30 days · top_countries field
🌍
Loading ACLED data…
⚔️ Disorder types i
ACLED disorder types
ACLED classifies events into 5 disorder types: Political violence (armed actors fighting), Demonstrations (protests, riots), Economic violence (targeting livelihoods), Strategic developments (non-violent but conflict-related). The breakdown reveals the nature of global conflict.
Source: ACLED · disorder_type field · 30 days
⚔️
Loading…
📡 Recent event feed — ACLED live i
ACLED live feed
Most recent events with geolocation, involved actors, and confirmed fatalities. Sorted by date descending. Configure ACLED_EMAIL + ACLED_PASSWORD in GitHub Secrets, then run the workflow to populate this feed with real geolocated data.
Source: ACLED · data/conflicts.json · live_feed field
💥
Configure ACLED_EMAIL + ACLED_PASSWORD in GitHub Secrets
then run the workflow to load live conflict data
🌍 World population — live counter (UN 2024 projections) i
World population counter
Extrapolated from UN 2024 projections: 4.5 births/second and 1.8 deaths/second, net +2.7 people/second. We add ~85 million people per year — equivalent to Germany every 12 months. Growth is now almost entirely in Sub-Saharan Africa.
Source: UN World Population Prospects 2024 · extrapolated in real-time

🏕 Forcibly displaced people — UNHCR i
UNHCR Forced Displacement
Total of refugees, internally displaced persons (IDPs), and asylum seekers worldwide. This figure has more than doubled in 10 years — from 43M in 2012 to 117M+ in 2024. It rises before economic crises, not after — a leading indicator of global instability.
Source: UNHCR · api.unhcr.org · Annual · data/refugees.json
117.3
million people (2024 est.)
↑ +2.9M vs. prev year · historical record
💚 Life expectancy at birth — top & bottom countries i
Life expectancy at birth
Number of years a newborn can expect to live, given current mortality rates. A composite measure of health, inequality, and development. The 32-year gap between Japan (84.3y) and the DRC (52y) reveals stark global inequality. Columns show change over 1 year and 10 years.
Source: WHO GHO / World Bank · Annual · WHO data 2024
Country Years Age +1Y +10Y
📊 Aging index — 65+ / working age ratio i
Aging Index
Number of people aged 65+ per 100 working-age people (15–64). Japan at 52 means 52 retirees for every 100 workers — the highest pension system pressure in the world. Above 35 = significant structural economic impact. Values from World Bank API (SP.POP.65UP.TO.ZS / SP.POP.1564.TO.ZS).
Source: World Bank · Annual · live data
📖 Wikipedia — top pages today i
Wikipedia pageviews
What humanity is collectively reading today, in real time. Spikes on pages like "Recession", "War", or "Pandemic" often precede mainstream news coverage by days. A collective consciousness detector updated daily by Wikimedia Analytics.
Source: Wikimedia REST API · wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1 · Daily
📖
Loading Wikimedia API…
🔬 arXiv — latest scientific papers i
arXiv preprints
Scientific papers posted before peer review. Volume of AI papers doubled the year before every major breakthrough announcement. arXiv is the best early-warning system for technological disruptions — typically 3–5 years before products reach market. Free, open access, no API key needed.
Source: arXiv.org · export.arxiv.org/api · Free · via corsproxy.io
🔬
Loading arXiv…
🏃 Physical activity — Strava Metro i
Strava Metro — global activity
Aggregate global estimates from Strava Year in Sport reports (2021–2024). Running and cycling volumes indexed to same period last year / 3 years ago. Strava Metro detailed real-time data requires institutional access.
Source: Strava Year in Sport · metro.strava.com · Annual
Global aggregate · 2B+ activities tracked · metro.strava.com
🍽 Restaurant & bar spending — FRED i
US Food Services & Drinking Places
Monthly retail sales for US restaurants and bars (NAICS 722). Seasonally adjusted. A direct proxy for consumer confidence and discretionary spending. Published monthly by the US Census Bureau via FRED (series MRTSSM7220USN). YoY % change shown.
Source: FRED · MRTSSM7220USN · US Census Bureau · Monthly
🍽
Loading FRED data…
🔍 Google Trends — top searches i
Google Trends RSS
What people are searching for right now, sourced from Google Trends daily trending topics RSS feed. Unlike polling, search trends capture anxiety and interest in real-time. Spikes in "recession" or "inflation" search terms are leading indicators of consumer sentiment shifts.
Source: Google Trends RSS · trends.google.com/trending/rss · Daily via corsproxy.io
Loading Google Trends…
